conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility properly, I need to gather some information about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 21 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand a bit more about their current situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me gather just a bit more detail to complete the assessment.

Do you live in the UK, and is Alex living with you or are you responsible for their care?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK, but Alex doesn't live with me anymore. They've moved out.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I need to read the official guidance to make a proper assessment. Let me check the rules now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I now have the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

One final question: Are you currently contributing to Alex's upkeep - for example, paying for things like clothes, food, pocket money, or contributing financially to their accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out a bit. I contribute about £20 a week towards their expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment of your circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16 to 19 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, they exceed the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility.

Although you are contributing £20 per week towards Alex's expenses, which is commendable, the age and education requirements are fundamental criteria that must be met. Unfortunately, Child Benefit is not available for children aged 20 or over, regardless of other circumstances such as financial contributions or living arrangements.

If you have any other questions or if Alex's circumstances change (though at 21, they would still be above the age limit), please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and live in the UK. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Therefore, Alex exceeds the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility. Even though you are contributing £20 per week towards Alex's expenses and Alex no longer lives with you, the age and education requirements are not met. Child Benefit is not available for children aged 20 or over, regardless of other circumstances.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in education or training, they exceed the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, and therefore the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Therefore, Alex exceeds the maximum age threshold for Child Benefit eligibility. Even though you are contributing £20 per week towards Alex's expenses and Alex no longer lives with you, the age and education requirements are not met. Child Benefit is not available for children aged 20 or over, regardless of other circumstances.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for those aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 and not in education or training, they exceed the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, and therefore the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
